semanticCore: Homeland, place of residence, station, battlefield, or domestic habitations

keyDerivations.meaning: Battlefield, place of standing, home, or station of combat

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي مَوَاطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ ۙ وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ (9:25): God has already given you victory in many battlefields/combat locations and on the day of Hunayn.
summaryEn: The root signifies places of station or battle locations (Mawatin), reminding the companions of the numerous geographic sites where God granted them military victories.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الوطن ومكان الإقامة ومواقف الحرب ومعارك القتال ومقامات النصر (وطن)، مذكراً بنصر الله للمسلمين في مواطن وغزوات كثيرة.
rawText: أَوْطَنَ 4 see 10. اِسْتَوْطَنَ 10 He took for himself as a home, or settled place of abode, (S, Msb, K,) a country; (S, Msb;) as also 🡻أَوْطَنَ (S, Msb, K) and 🡻وَطَّنَ (S, K) or 🡻تَوَطَّنَ. (Msb.) تَوَطَّنَتْ نَفْسُهُ عَلَى الشَّىْءِ 5 , and لَهُ, He undertook the thing, and submitted to it; syn. تَحَمَّلَتْ وَذَلَّتْ لَهُ (ISd, in TA) [see also 2 and 10] his mind, or he, became disposed and subjected to do the thing; syn. تَمَهَّدَتْ. (K [in the CK, for تَوَطُّنَهَا تَمَهُّدَهَا, is put تَوَطَّنَهَا تَمَهَّدَهَا].) وَطَنٌ The place of abode or residence (S, Mgh, K) of a man: (S, Mgh:) a man's settled place of abode; his place of constant residence; his dwelling; his home. (Msb) وَطَّنَ نَفْسَهُ عَلَى الأَمْرِ 2 He disposed and subjected his mind, or himself, to do the thing; syn. مَهَّدَهَا لِفِعْلِهِ وَذَلَّلَهَا (Msb.) === وظّن نفسه على الشَّىْءِ, and لَهُ, He induced, or persuaded, himself to do the thing; syn. حَمَلَهَا عَلَيْهِ. (ISd, in TA.) See also 5 and 10.
